params.name: Hurrian Songs
params.description:
On May 2, 1374 BC, the Hurrian Songs were presented to the city of Ugarit for its people to sing.
The Hurrian Songs are a collection of songs inscribed in cuneiform on clay tablets from the ancient city of Ugarit in 1400 BC. The song consists of two parts: one part is a hymn in praise of the goddess of the orchard, and the other part is instructions for how to play the nine-stringed lyre. The Hurrian Songs are the world's oldest music score that is relatively well-preserved.
